characters.physical_details: Lean, sun-weathered, scar-handed, with short graying hair and the economical movement of someone used to low levels.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: guide, manipulator, then damaged crossing partner
key_relationships.maren: former lover who crossed eight years earlier
key_relationships.thessa: former partner, estranged for twelve years

characters.occupation_status: Freelance healer and route fixer taking the jobs the Hoarded abandon.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate; reads common script and Hoarded paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Uses 2 healing levels on Hael and starts north at level 31 with Maren's route journal.
Drops to 28 after dismantling the first retrieval team with precise combat spending.
Drops to 22 and takes cracked left ribs during the grain-silo fight.
Drops to 20 escaping Thessa's settlement; Thessa exposes Vel's earlier search for a high-level cascade partner.
Drops to 18 in the Margins while Kem burns through the reserve she meant for the Scour.
At 14, finally tells Kem that Maren crossed eight years earlier and that the journey is an attempt to follow her.
Drops to 8 scouting the Scour and navigating the mining tunnel; learns the threshold has been deliberately degraded.
Spends to zero at the threshold and crosses into the sub-Ledger.

characters.availability: Present throughout; above-ground until chapter 18, then alive in the sub-Ledger.

characters.physical_details: Tall and initially soft from indoor aristocratic life; becomes sunburned, bruised, callused, and leaner on the road.

key_relationships.vel_ashwick: guide, teacher, manipulator, then explicit co-traveler under damaged trust

characters.occupation_status: House Aldraste heir at the start; by the end, a zeroed arrival in the sub-Ledger.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in common and Hoarded formal script; educated but practically untrained at the start.

state_transitions.change: Leaves the Vault, burns 12 levels in his first hours outside, and takes the House signet; ends at 288.
Falls from 286 to 245 in his first real fight, discovering both exhilaration and catastrophic overspending.
Drops to 183 using an oversized defensive perimeter during the grain-silo fight.
Drops to 160 during the escape from Thessa's settlement and learns Vel had searched for a high-level partner before finding him.
Falls from 148 to 50 in the Margins by healing, breaking a labor-compound wall, fighting enforcers, and repairing infrastructure.
Reaches 31 after a road-collapse rescue, learns he was recruited as cascade fuel, and removes the signet from his hand.
Drops to 25 rescuing a family from a collapsing house; understands the appeal of preservation and rejects it.
At 18, hears the full Maren story and keeps walking south on explicit terms.
Drops to 12 getting Vel through the mining tunnel and onto the Scour floor.
Falls from 12 to 0 through the final shield, motion spend, and last two-level push; crosses into the sub-Ledger.
Learns to live without the Ledger's hum and leaves the Aldraste signet on a windowsill.

characters.availability: Present from chapter 2 onward; crosses in chapter 18.

characters.physical_details: Administratively maintained at the start; by late book she is road-worn, callused, chapped, and visibly changed by field life.

key_relationships.kem_aldraste: retrieval target turned subject of sympathy
key_relationships.hoarded_council: institution she serves and ultimately deceives

characters.occupation_status: Senior Auditor of the Hoarded Council, then an independent actor shaped by the pursuit.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate; expert in Hoarded records and procedure.

state_transitions.change: Uses Wayfinder for the first time, drops to 298, and leaves the Vault leading an untrained team.
At 270 after six weeks outside, lies to Lady Aldraste about being 285 and starts asking whether spending makes a person more.
At 238, sends the remaining agents back, has already filed a false natural-depletion report, and warns Vel and Kem about the Scour garrison.
At 236, commits to the descent after reading that the degraded threshold needs three contributors.
Burns 7 levels to steady the failing threshold, leaving herself at 229, but stays above zero.
Reaffirms the cover story: official record remains level-zero termination with no threshold event.

characters.availability: POV in chapters 6 and 15, intercut later, present at the climax, and still above the threshold at the end.

characters.physical_details: Stocky, road-darkened trader with callused hands; after chapter 8 he carries a badly healed left-arm gash.

key_relationships.vel_ashwick: paid guide who grows into an ally
key_relationships.mira: daughter whose legs need expensive treatment

characters.occupation_status: Southern-route trader working toward Mira's bone-treatment costs in Roth.
characters.literacy_languages: Functionally literate; common tongue with trader literacy.

state_transitions.change: Joins the journey at level 138 after negotiating a double-run guide fee.
Takes a deep left-forearm wound from silo stone.
Kem stitches the arm badly but well enough to keep it functional.
At about 130, keeps traveling past his original contract after watching Kem spend on Margins strangers.
Turns back north at about 120 for Mira; names her as his own zero and leaves with the arm healed badly.

characters.availability: Present from chapter 7 through chapter 16, then gone north before the Scour.

characters.physical_details: Sturdy, settled, and weathered; her body reads as stationary labor rather than road wear.

key_relationships.vel_ashwick: former partner, estranged for twelve years
key_relationships.hoarded_representatives: source of the bargain she accepted to protect the settlement

characters.occupation_status: Settlement householder and low-spend trader living at level 78 under a Hoarded reporting arrangement.

state_transitions.change: Gives route information, reveals Vel's earlier search for high-level defectors, and admits she traded traveler intelligence for grain, timber, healer visits, and medicine.

characters.physical_details: Gaunt and deeply weathered, moving with the careful conservation of a level-4 life.
characters.occupation_status: Former field-debt laborer, sent out at 140 and reduced to 4 after thirty years of Hoarded 'development' work.
characters.availability: Present in chapter 12 as the deep-Margins witness to the Scour route and prior vanishings.

characters.physical_details: Formally immaculate Hoarded aristocrat, still fully present behind an unspent level-300 body.
characters.occupation_status: Matriarch of House Aldraste and de facto active head of house politics while Lord Aldraste declines.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in Hoarded formal script.
characters.availability: Present in chapter 2 and by communication thereafter.

characters.physical_details: Physically perfect at level 300 but mentally thinned by advanced Stillness: open eyes, formal posture, delayed speech.
characters.occupation_status: Patriarch of House Aldraste in title only; functionally absent under Stillness.
characters.literacy_languages: Formerly fully literate; current cognitive function is degraded.
characters.availability: Present in chapter 2 and referenced throughout as Kem's reason for leaving.

characters.physical_details: Not fully described until chapter 19; older than when Vel last saw her, healthy, and outside the Ledger.

key_relationships.vel_ashwick: former lover reunited after eight years

characters.occupation_status: Former deep-route explorer; by chapter 19 she serves on the southern district committee for infrastructure and intake.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate; she kept the shared route journal that Vel carries.

state_transitions.change: Reunites with Vel after eight years and receives Kem in the lower settlement.

characters.availability: Referenced from chapter 1 onward; physically present in chapter 19.

timeline.story_start: Late spring, in a small farming settlement south of the Vault, just after Vel heals Hael.
timeline.estimated_span: Approximately six to eight weeks from rumor to threshold crossing.

seasonal_track.season: Late spring moving into early summer.
Early summer through the Spent Towns, river boundary, and first Margins settlements.
High summer heat across the deep Margins, the Scour, and the first day below the threshold.

key_events.event: Vel heals Hael to level 31, hears of Kem's defection, and heads north with Maren's route journal.
Kem leaves the Vault, burns 12 levels in his first hours outside, and takes the House signet.
Dren joins; the grain-silo fight wounds Dren and Vel and drives Kem to 183.
In the Margins, Kem destroys a labor-compound wall, spends 98 levels helping strangers and infrastructure, and Fallow confirms the Scour disappearances.
Sable warns that trained Red Reserve fighters and a permanent Scour posting are waiting ahead.
key_events.timing: About two days before the Scour

key_events.event: Commander Osen reveals the threshold was deliberately damped and that Vel's old two-person math is obsolete.
key_events.timing: Day of arrival at the Scour

key_events.event: Vel reaches zero, Sable steadies the tear with seven levels, and Kem spends his last two levels to cross.
Kem wakes in the sub-Ledger, Maren receives Vel in the lower settlement, and Sable files the lie that protects the crossing.
key_events.timing: Same day or later that night

geography.primary_setting: A north-to-south road from the Vault through the Spent Towns and the depleted Margins to the Scour, then first arrival in the sub-Ledger.

key_locations.name: Northern farming settlement
key_locations.details: Vel's starting point in chapter 1: rust-fever losses, thin labor, and payment in marks and barter.

key_locations.name: The Vault
key_locations.details: Hoarded capital and seat of House Aldraste, including the Conservatory Rite, ceremonial gate, and sealed eastern wing tied to the Red Reserve.

key_locations.name: Spent Towns
key_locations.details: Working settlements that openly spend on roads, farming, healing, and repairs; Dren is hired here.

key_locations.name: Thessa's settlement
key_locations.details: South of the Spent Towns; site of Vel's old ties, Thessa's reporting bargain, and a Hoarded representative already showing Stillness.

key_locations.name: River boundary
key_locations.details: The old bridge south of Thessa's settlement is gone; the ford marks the practical boundary between the Spent territories and the Margins.

key_locations.name: The Margins
key_locations.details: Chalk-pale depleted country of failing Shaping, productivity notices, debt labor compounds, and settlements living under Hoarded extraction logic.

key_locations.name: The Scour
key_locations.details: Wind-scoured erosion basin with a permanent Red Reserve camp and the threshold point on the floor; see spatial layout for micro geography.

key_locations.name: The sub-Ledger valley settlements
key_locations.details: Hand-built stone communities reached through the threshold, including intake, infirmary, council hall, terraces, and Maren's lower settlement.

world_rules: Everyone is born at level 300. Ability use permanently spends levels; there is no known restoration.
Basic human effort costs nothing. The Ledger prices enhanced abilities, not walking, eating, labor, or ordinary pain.
People feel their own remaining level as a background hum. The absence of that hum is the first shock of the sub-Ledger.
The Hoarded build status around never spending. Long-term preservation at 300 produces Stillness, a degenerative loss of presence that follows defectors too.
Spent Town life depends on routine useful expenditure; the Margins show the same system run as extraction.
Level zero is not automatically death. At the Scour and similar sites, zero can open a passage out of the Ledger world.
Thresholds work by cascade: more nearby expenditure and a larger descent produce a stronger crossing.
The Hoarded inner leadership knows the threshold and sub-Ledger are real and suppresses that truth through secrecy, the Red Reserve, and dampening.
By chapter 17 the Scour has degraded enough that two contributors are unreliable; Sable's report says a stable event now needs at least three.
The sub-Ledger has no Ledger, no levels, and no abilities. It runs on manual work, medicine, and council governance.

power_structure.over: The Ledger world's land, trade, official truth, and district policy
power_structure.mechanism: Hereditary rank, contracts, debt systems, labor policy, and control of the story the Hoarded tell about spending and zero.
De facto leadership while Lord Aldraste declines under Stillness.

power_structure.over: Margins settlements, labor compounds, and traveler intelligence
power_structure.mechanism: Productivity tiers, ability-debt, quota review, labor enforcement, and bargaining for information.
Permanent garrison, trained combat spending, and site dampening ordered by the Hoarded.
Practical self-governance sustained by everyday productive spending.

power_structure.over: Infrastructure, intake, labor, and resource disputes below the threshold
power_structure.mechanism: Committee and council decisions in a society with no Ledger, including tension between voluntary arrivals and long-standing zero communities.

objects.name: Vel's Ledger
objects.status: Starts at 33, drops to 31 in chapter 1, and reaches 0 at the threshold in chapter 18.

objects.name: Kem's Ledger
objects.status: Starts at 300, becomes the book's main expendable reserve, and reaches 0 in chapter 18.

objects.name: Kem's House signet
objects.origin: House Aldraste ceremonial stand at the Vault gate
objects.status: Taken when he leaves, worn through most of the journey, pocketed after chapter 13, and left on a sub-Ledger windowsill in chapter 19.

objects.name: Maren's route journal
objects.origin: Shared field journal from Vel and Maren's route years
objects.status: Vel's main route source and emotional anchor; two handwritings; carries the Scour notes and marked threshold point.

objects.name: Margins productivity notices
objects.origin: Hoarded district administration board in Margins District 7, Sector 14-B
objects.status: Pulled from a notice board in chapter 12 and carried south as evidence of the Margins debt regime.

debts.amount: Marks equivalent to forty levels of bone-work for Mira's legs; the previous payment covered about half
debts.deadline: Ongoing; Dren turns back in chapter 16 to keep earning toward it
debts.status: Still underfunded when Dren leaves the group

debts.creditor: Hoarded Bureau of Expenditure Oversight and district offices
debts.amount: Margins tiered ability-debt: Tier 1 owes 0.3 levels per year, Tier 2 0.15; Tier 3 can be reassigned as 'Renewable Expenditure Units'; Tier 4 faces account closure; appeals cost 2 levels
debts.deadline: Recurring district review and labor reassignment schedule
debts.status: Systemic debt structure imposed on Margins residents

income_sources.source: Vel's freelance healing and repair work
income_sources.amount: Irregular small payments in marks or barter
Run-by-run trader income; a southern guide fee is roughly a double eastern run
income_sources.reliability: Reliable when he can stay on route

income_sources.amount: Finite silver fittings, clasps, and other carried value converted into marks on the road
Grain priority, timber, healer visits twice a season, and medicine during lung-fever outbreaks
income_sources.reliability: Reliable only while she keeps supplying route intelligence

knowledge_state.knows: Level zero at the Scour is a real threshold; Maren crossed there eight years earlier; and a stable crossing needs a high-level cascade partner, which is why Vel searched for defectors and recruited Kem.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Eight years before story start and the investigation that followed
hidden_from: kem_aldraste until chapters 09, 13, and 16 depending on which part of the truth is in question
everyone else for most of the book

knowledge_state.knows: Stillness is degenerative rot rather than virtue; by chapter_15 he also knows the damage follows people who leave the Vault.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Before story start; expanded in chapter_15

knowledge_state.knows: The Hoarded bought traveler intelligence from her with grain, timber, healer visits, and medicine, and Vel had already been searching for high-level defectors years before Kem.

The Red Reserve exists; the Scour has a permanent garrison; the threshold was deliberately damped; she files a false natural-depletion report by chapter_15; and after chapter_18 keeps the official story at 'level-zero termination, no threshold event observed.'
knowledge_state.learned_in: chapter_06 and later record access during the pursuit
hidden_from: the official record after the crossing

knowledge_state.knows: The threshold and sub-Ledger are real and worth suppressing; this is why the Hoarded keep a Scour garrison and damp the site.
hidden_from: most people in the Spent Towns and the Margins

knowledge_state.knows: People who hit zero at the Scour disappear with no body left behind; Fallow personally saw Sera and Hoster vanish.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Observed over decades in the Margins
hidden_from: not hidden within the deep Margins, but largely unknown to the Hoarded and most Spent Towns people

knowledge_state.knows: The threshold has degraded enough that two contributors are no longer reliable, and she has personally watched three prior crossings.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Five years of permanent station and classified reporting before chapter_17

knowledge_state.knows: The people who vanished at the Scour survive in a functioning sub-Ledger community; Sera, one of Fallow's vanished crossers, is alive there and works intake.

environmental_constants: Above zero, people feel the Ledger as a background hum; its absence defines the sub-Ledger.
The Vault is unnaturally quiet and built to make stillness feel virtuous.
Spent Towns sound alive with practical ability use: farming hum, healing pulses, and repair work.
The Margins are chalk-pale, dry, overused, and cluttered with failing Shaping and bureaucratic residue.
The Scour is all wind, exposed layered rock, heat off bare stone, and almost no cover.
The sub-Ledger shows labor in every surface: tool-marked stone, terraces, obsidian medicine, and infrastructure built without abilities.
